# function: ip_add

Returns the IP address shifted by `n` (positive to advance, negative to go back). Supports both IPv4 and IPv6. Returns an error if the result would overflow the address space.

**Common uses:** computing gateway addresses (`ip_add(cidr_first_ip(var.subnet), 1)`), deriving DNS server IPs, enumerating specific host addresses without needing a full CIDR context.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// Conventional addresses derived from a subnet base.
locals {
  subnet_base = provider::burnham::cidr_first_ip("10.0.1.0/24") // "10.0.1.0"
  gateway     = provider::burnham::ip_add(local.subnet_base, 1) // "10.0.1.1"
  dns         = provider::burnham::ip_add(local.subnet_base, 2) // "10.0.1.2"
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
ip_add(ip string, n number) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `ip` (String) The base IP address.
1. `n` (Number) The integer offset to apply (may be negative).
